narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a punch to the gut, especially when you've poured so much into getting here. Our prediction model looked at your full story and estimated a 46% chance you'd struggle to pass the bar exam down the line, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 2.0, sitting in the bottom tenth of your class—that was the biggest signal to the model that the rigors of legal training might prove overwhelming in the long haul. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 37.5, which didn't quite signal the raw analytical firepower the model associates with bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 2.0 echoed that early pattern, suggesting consistency in a challenging academic environment rather than a quick upward climb. Even with your high family income offering a safety net, it oddly factored against you, perhaps painting a picture of less urgency or grit in the model's eyes. Your white racial background and male gender nudged things further toward caution, aligning with patterns the model has seen before. Your solid 3.3 undergrad GPA and full-time commitment pulled in the same worrisome direction, not strong enough to counterbalance the rest. Together, these pieces formed a mosaic that just didn't inspire confidence in your bar passage odds. It's not the end of your story, though—many with similar starts have pivoted, retaken tests, or found other paths to shine. We truly wish you the best as you chart your next steps.
